title: काली चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
कालिका माता चरन रज, शीश धरत कर जोड़।
चालीसा कहूँ भक्ति से, करो कृपा अति होड़॥
महाकाली जगदम्ब तू, रक्तबीज संहार।
करो कृपा दासन पर, हरो भवन का भार॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय महाकाली जगदम्बे।
जय काली माँ सुखदायक अम्बे॥
श्यामा वरन विकराल स्वरूपा।
मुण्डमाला शिर अति अनूपा॥
लम्बी जिह्वा लाल बिराजे।
खप्पर खड्ग करन में साजे॥
शिव शरीर पर चरण तिहारे।
सकल दैत्य गण भय से हारे॥
रक्तबीज का रुधिर पीया।
महादैत्य का नाश किया॥
चामुण्डा रूप धरा विकरालू।
चण्ड मुण्ड का किया कबालू॥
शुम्भ निशुम्भ विनाश किया।
जगत को भय से मुक्ति दिया॥
दक्षिण काली रूप सुहावन।
शमशान भूमि निवास तुम्हारन॥
महाविद्या दस में प्रथम।
काली माता सर्वोत्तम॥
कालरात्रि नवदुर्गा रूपा।
सातवें दिन पूजा अनूपा॥
तन्त्र साधना की तुम स्वामी।
शक्ति पूजा की तुम अंतर्यामी॥
कालिका पुराण में बखानी।
महाशक्ति कालिका कल्याणी॥
कालीघाट में मंदिर भारी।
जहाँ बिराजे कालिका प्यारी॥
दक्षिणेश्वर का धाम सुहाना।
रामकृष्ण ने नाम पुकारा जाना॥
वामा खेपा ने सेवा कीन्हा।
माँ काली ने दर्शन दीन्हा॥
तारापीठ में रूप विराजे।
साधक जन मन मंगल साजे॥
बंगाल की माता तू कहलाई।
सकल शक्तिपीठ में समाई॥
नर बलि का अब नहीं विधाना।
भक्ति भाव से करो पुराणा॥
माँ काली से शक्ति पावे।
भक्त जनन को मुक्ति करावे॥
त्रिकाल दर्शिनी माता तू ही।
काल से काल मिटावे तू ही॥
भूत प्रेत पिशाच भगावे।
काली नाम जो कोई ध्यावे॥
शत्रु नाश करो महारानी।
सब विपत हरो कल्याणी॥
सकल मनोरथ पूरण कीजे।
भक्तन को अभय वर दीजे॥
नवरात्रि में पूजन होवे।
काली माता दर्शन जोवे॥
दीपावली काली पूजा रात।
बंगाल में मनाये जगजात॥
जो यह चालीसा नित गावे।
सो सब भय संकट मिटावे॥
काली माता के चरण में आवे।
सो परम पद निश्चय पावे॥
शक्ति प्राप्त हो शत्रु विनाशे।
काली कृपा से पाप प्रनाशे॥
चालीसा पढ़ विनय सुनावे।
माँ काली सब कष्ट मिटावे॥

॥ दोहा ॥
काली चालीसा पढ़े, भक्ति भाव मन धार।
सकल विपत का नाश हो, माँ करें भव पार॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Kalika Mata Charan Raj, Shish Dharat Kar Jod.
Chalisa Kahun Bhakti Se, Karo Kripa Ati Hod.
Mahakali Jagdamb Tu, Raktabeej Sanhar.
Karo Kripa Dasan Par, Haro Bhavan Ka Bhar.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Mahakali Jagdambe.
Jai Kali Maa Sukhdayak Ambe.
Shyama Varan Vikral Swaroopa.
Mundmala Shir Ati Anoopa.
Lambi Jihva Laal Biraje.
Khappar Khadg Karan Mein Saje.
Shiv Sharir Par Charan Tihare.
Sakal Daitya Gan Bhay Se Hare.
Raktabeej Ka Rudhir Piya.
Mahadaitya Ka Nash Kiya.
Chamunda Roop Dhara Vikralu.
Chand Mund Ka Kiya Kabalu.
Shumbh Nishumbh Vinash Kiya.
Jagat Ko Bhay Se Mukti Diya.
Dakshin Kali Roop Suhavan.
Shamshan Bhumi Nivas Tumharan.
Mahavidya Das Mein Pratham.
Kali Mata Sarvottam.
Kalratri Navdurga Roopa.
Satven Din Puja Anoopa.
Tantra Sadhana Ki Tum Swami.
Shakti Puja Ki Tum Antaryami.
Kalika Puran Mein Bakhani.
Mahashakti Kalika Kalyani.
Kalighat Mein Mandir Bhari.
Jahan Biraje Kalika Pyari.
Dakshineshwar Ka Dham Suhana.
Ramkrishna Ne Naam Pukara Jana.
Vama Khepa Ne Seva Kinha.
Maa Kali Ne Darshan Dinha.
Tarapeeth Mein Roop Viraje.
Sadhak Jan Man Mangal Saje.
Bangal Ki Mata Tu Kahlai.
Sakal Shaktipeeth Mein Samai.
Nar Bali Ka Ab Nahi Vidhana.
Bhakti Bhav Se Karo Purana.
Maa Kali Se Shakti Pave.
Bhakt Janan Ko Mukti Karave.
Trikal Darshini Mata Tu Hi.
Kal Se Kal Mitave Tu Hi.
Bhoot Pret Pishach Bhagave.
Kali Naam Jo Koi Dhyave.
Shatru Nash Karo Maharani.
Sab Vipat Haro Kalyani.
Sakal Manorath Puran Kije.
Bhaktan Ko Abhay Var Dije.
Navratri Mein Pujan Hove.
Kali Mata Darshan Jove.
Deepawali Kali Puja Raat.
Bangal Mein Manaye Jagjat.
Jo Yah Chalisa Nit Gave.
So Sab Bhay Sankat Mitave.
Kali Mata Ke Charan Mein Ave.
So Param Pad Nishchay Pave.
Shakti Prapt Ho Shatru Vinashe.
Kali Kripa Se Paap Pranashe.
Chalisa Padh Vinay Sunave.
Maa Kali Sab Kasht Mitave.

|| Doha ||
Kali Chalisa Padhe, Bhakti Bhav Man Dhar.
Sakal Vipat Ka Nash Ho, Maa Karen Bhav Par.
hindiMeaning:
कालिका माता के चरण रज को शीश पर धारण कर हाथ जोड़कर भक्ति से चालीसा कहता हूँ। महाकाली जगदम्बे! रक्तबीज का संहार करने वाली! दासों पर कृपा करो और भवसागर का भार हरो।

जय महाकाली जगदम्बे! श्यामा वर्ण विकराल स्वरूपा, मुण्डमाला धारण किये अत्यंत अनुपम! शिव के शरीर पर चरण रखे, सकल दैत्यगण भयभीत हुए।
benefits: Provides powerful protection against enemies and evil forces
Traditionally recited for removal of fear, negativity, and black magic effects
Invokes immense courage and inner strength
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles caused by malefic planetary influences
Special significance during Deepawali Kali Puja in Bengal
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Kali Chalisa — काली चालीसा — is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn offered to Goddess Kali. Rooted in Traditional Chalisa Sangrah, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to destroy fear, negativity, and black magic effects, provide powerful protection against enemies and evil forces, and grant immense courage and inner strength. This prayer is traditionally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Navratri, Kali Puja, and Diwali to invoke divine grace. With 42 verses and a reading time of about 15 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Kali Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Kali Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Powerful prayer to Goddess Kali for protection and destruction of evil.

seoFaqs.question: How does the Kali Chalisa describe the goddess's appearance?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kali Chalisa describes Maa Kali in her terrifying yet liberating iconography — dark or jet-black complexion (krishna-varna), disheveled hair (mukta-keshi), garland of severed heads (mundamala), girdle of severed arms, protruding tongue, and standing on the prone body of Shiva. As Hindu Mirror notes, 'her appearance is terrifying yet she is the most compassionate mother to her devotees.' The Chalisa repeatedly contrasts this fierce form with her tender protective grace, teaching that the same power that destroys evil shelters her bhaktas. (Source: Hindu Mirror Kali Chalisa; Divine Aarti Kali Chalisa.)

seoFaqs.question: Why are Tuesdays and Saturdays prescribed for Kali Chalisa recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: In Hindu astrological tradition, Tuesday (Mangalvar) is ruled by Mangal (Mars) and Saturday (Shanivar) by Shani (Saturn) — both grahas associated with discipline, protection from enemies, and dissolution of obstacles. These match Kali's role as destroyer of negativity. Hindu Mirror specifies that Kali Chalisa 'is particularly powerful when recited on Tuesdays, Saturdays, and during Kali Puja.' Bengali Shakta tradition adds Amavasya (new moon) nights as the most potent, and gupt-Navaratri (the secret Navaratri in Magha and Ashadha) for esoteric practitioners. (Source: Hindu Mirror Kali Chalisa; Mantra.tips Kali Chalisa benefits.)

seoFaqs.question: What protection does the Kali Chalisa specifically promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional commentaries highlight three categories of protection: (1) external — from enemies, hostile sorcery (abhichara), evil spirits, theft, and accidents; (2) internal — from deep-seated fear, ego, ignorance, and depression; (3) karmic — from the binding effect of past deeds. ChantChalisa.com summarises: 'Chanting Kali Chalisa provides fierce protection from enemies, evil spirits, and destructive forces. It removes deep-seated fears, destroys ignorance and ego, and bestows fearlessness and liberation.' Devotees facing court cases, illness, or hostile situations frequently take a 40-day Kali Chalisa anushthana. (Source: Chant Chalisa Maa Kali Chalisa; HinduMirror Kali Chalisa.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Kali Chalisa connected to the Bengali Kali Puja?
seoFaqs.answer: Kali Puja in Bengal is celebrated on the Amavasya of Kartik month — the same night as Lakshmi Puja in northern India for Diwali. Devotees at Kalighat (Kolkata), Dakshineswar (Ramakrishna's temple), Tarapith, and Kamakhya recite the Kali Chalisa alongside the Karpuradi Stotra, Mahishasura-Mardini Stotram, and verses from the Devi Mahatmya. The Chalisa, being in simple Hindi/Awadhi rather than Sanskrit, is the most accessible text and is sung publicly during the night-long jagran. (Source: Drikpanchang Mahakali Chalisa; Bhagwat Geeta Maa Kali Chalisa.)

seoFaqs.question: Is there a specific time of night to chant the Kali Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. Hindu Mirror and other sources state that 'nighttime recitation, especially midnight, is considered the most potent time for invoking Kali Maa's blessings.' This is rooted in the tantric understanding that Kali presides over the nishitha-kala (the dead of night), corresponding to her cosmic role as the goddess of mahapralaya — the great dissolution. Householders typically chant before sleep with a single oil lamp, while serious sadhakas observe the full Brahma-muhurta-to-midnight kala-rahasya practice. The Chalisa is always preceded by a guru-pranama and Ganesha vandana. (Source: Hindu Mirror Kali Chalisa; Ritiriwaz Kali Chalisa.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Kali Chalisa fit into Shakta sadhana?
seoFaqs.answer: Within the broader Shri Vidya / Dasha-Mahavidya tradition, Kali is the first and supreme of the Ten Wisdom Goddesses (Adi Mahavidya). The Chalisa serves as the entry-level upasana — recited daily for stability and protection before a sadhaka takes formal initiation (diksha) into the Kali mantra of fifteen syllables (panchadashakshari) or the longer Krim-mantra. Mantra.tips frames it as 'the gateway hymn that prepares the chitta (mind-stuff) to bear Kali's higher mantras safely.' This pedagogical role explains why the Chalisa is valued even in tantric circles that otherwise prefer Sanskrit hymns. (Source: Mantra.tips Maa Kali Chalisa; Devi RedZambala Mahakali Chalisa.)
